faqs.q: What does autism spectrum disorder mean?
faqs.a: Autism spectrum disorder is a developmental difference in how the brain is wired that affects how a person communicates, builds relationships, and takes in sensory information. It's present from early childhood and shows up in a wide range of ways from one person to the next.
No. Autism is not caused by vaccines, parenting, or anything a person did. Decades of research have settled that question.

faqs.q: Is autism the same as an intellectual disability?
faqs.a: No, though the two can occur together. Many autistic people have average or above-average intelligence. Being shy, introverted, or socially awkward also isn't the same as being autistic, which is a specific developmental pattern a trained clinician evaluates.
